2605.19815.title: LP-Eval: Rubric and Dataset for Measuring the Quality of Legal Proposition Generation
2605.19815.paper_summary: Legal proposition generation is central to legal reasoning and doctrinal scholarship, yet remain under-examined in Legal NLP. This paper investigates the automatic generation and evaluation of legal propositions from decisions of the Court of Justice of the European Union using large language models (LLMs). We introduce LP-Eval, a three-step evaluation rubric co-designed with legal experts that decomposes legal proposition quality into formal validity and substantive dimensions. Using this rubric, we release a dataset of two experts' annotations for 100 LLM-generated legal propositions. Our results show that LLMs can generate predominantly well-formed and high-quality propositions, while expert evaluations reveal higher quality for propositions derived from well established cases than from recent ones. We further examine LLMs as evaluators and find that rubric-guided LLM judgments align more closely with expert assessments than direct overall scoring, but remain insensitive to finer-grained distinctions captured by human experts.
2605.19815.paper_summary_zh: 法律命題生成是法律推理和學術研究的核心，但在法律自然語言處理中仍然未受到充分檢視。本文研究了如何使用大型語言模型（LLMs）自動生成和評估歐洲聯盟法院的裁決中的法律命題。我們介紹了LP-Eval，一個與法律專家共同設計的三步評估標準，將法律命題的質量分解為形式有效性和實質維度。使用這個標準，我們釋放了一個包含兩位專家對100個LLM生成的法律命題註釋的數據集。我們的結果顯示，LLMs可以生成主要是良好構造和高質量的命題，而專家評估則顯示，源自成熟案例的命題質量高於近期案例。我們進一步檢視LLMs作為評估者，發現按照標準指導的LLM判斷與專家評估的吻合度高於直接的整體評分，但對人類專家捕捉的細微區別仍然不敏感。
2605.19815.author: Shanshan Xu et.al.
2605.19815.authors: Shanshan Xu, Johan Lindholm, Amogh Raina, Henrik Palmer Olsen, Daniel Hershcovich
